svg.text: JAPANESE — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
SERIES 18D OF 50 · REMEMBER FORWARD
— PASSAGE 1 — GREETING AND INTRODUCTION —
はじめまして。わたしは にんげんです。
Hajimemashite. Watashi wa ningen desu.
How-do-you-do I TOPIC human am/be-POLITE
"How do you do. I am a human being."
ことばは きおくです。きおくは みらいへの てがみです。
Kotoba wa kioku desu. Kioku wa mirai e-no tegami desu.
Language TOP memory be. Memory TOP future to-GEN letter be.
"Language is memory. Memory is a letter to the future."
— PASSAGE 2 — NATURE PASSAGE —
やまは たかく、かわは ふかい。
Yama wa takaku, kawa wa fukai.
Mountain TOP tall-CONJ river TOP deep.
"The mountain is high, the river is deep."
はるに はなが さき、ふゆに ゆきが ふる。
Haru-ni hana-ga saki, fuyu-ni yuki-ga furu.
Spring-LOC flower-SUBJ bloom, winter-LOC snow-SUBJ fall.
"In spring flowers bloom; in winter snow falls."
— PASSAGE 3 — TIME AND MEMORY (CLASSICAL STYLE) —
ゆく かわの ながれは たえずして…
Yuku kawa-no nagare wa taezu-shite…
Going river-GEN flow TOP cease-not-CONJ…
"The flow of a passing river never ceases…" — Kamo no Chōmei, Hōjōki (1212)
むかしの ひとの こころも、いまの わたしたちの こころも、おなじ。
Mukashi-no hito-no kokoro mo, ima-no watashitachi-no kokoro mo, onaji.
Old-GEN person-GEN heart also, now-GEN we-GEN heart also, same.
"The hearts of people of old and the hearts of us today are the same."
— PASSAGE 4 — WISDOM SAYING —
なせばなる。なさねばならぬ。なにごとも。
Naseba naru. Nasaneba naranu. Nanigoto mo.
Do-if become. Do-not-if become-not. Any-thing also.
"If you try, you can. If you do not try, you cannot. In anything." — Uesugi Yōzan
— BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
この ことばは あなたのために、みらいを おぼえた ひとびとによって、じゆうに つくられた。
Kono kotoba wa anata-no tame-ni, mirai-wo oboeta hitobito-ni-yotte, jiyū-ni tsukureta.
This word/message TOP you-GEN for-PURP, future-ACC remembered people-by, freely made-was.
"This message was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN JAPANESE —
Japanese expresses "forward in time" as 未来 mirai (not-yet-arrived) or 将来 shōrai (soon-coming).
mirai 未来: "not-yet + arrived" — the future as what has not yet come; used for distant/abstract future.
shōrai 将来: "about to + come" — the future as what is on its way; personal future, career, near-term.
The phrase uses おぼえた oboeta (remembered, past tense) with 未来 mirai — a deliberate paradox:
"people who remembered the future" — echoing the project name: Remember Forward (未来へ向けて記憶する).
に よって ni yotte: "by means of / by (agent)" — the grammatical marker for passive agency in Japanese.
じゆうに jiyū-ni: "freely" — literally "free + ADV" — freedom from cost and from constraint.
— DECODING NOTE — FOR FUTURE READERS —
If the kana symbols are unfamiliar: consult Plate 18A for the full hiragana and katakana syllabary tables.
Each symbol represents one syllable (mora). Read left to right, top to bottom in modern printed text.
Romaji (Latin-alphabet transcription) is provided line-by-line above for all passages on this plate.
Japanese is spoken by ~125 million people, primarily in Japan. It is a language isolate — no confirmed relatives.
SERIES 18 · JAPANESE · PLATE D · TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
